Title: A peroxisomal heterodimeric enzyme is involved in benzaldehyde synthesis in plants

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: As part of a heterodimeric benzaldehyde synthase with PhBSβ, reduces benzoyl-CoA to benzaldehyde using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting at enzyme level; expression of PhBSα likely provides key transcriptional control of benzaldehyde biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pollinator attractant; flavor volatile; antifungal compound
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Floral volatile emission; in petunia flowers most internal benzaldehyde pool accumulates in the cuticle
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes benzaldehyde production and emission; silencing together with PhBSβ reduces benzaldehyde, benzylalcohol and benzylbenzoate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in petal limbs and tubes; low in leaves and sepals; developmentally induced from buds to day 2 post-anthesis; rhythmic with peak before 19:00, preceding benzaldehyde emission
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in scent-emitting floral tissues and at times of peak scent production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Partial purification; proteomics identification; recombinant protein reconstitution with β subunit; enzyme assay; substrate specificity assay; pull-down; Y2H; BiFC; VIGS; transient pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Daily light/dark cycle; floral developmental stage; phenylalanine feeding in transient expression assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced benzaldehyde redirected flux toward phenylethylbenzoate and lowered phenylacetaldehyde/phenylethanol emission; no effect on methylbenzoate emission reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to engineer natural benzaldehyde flavor/aroma production; should be co-expressed with PhBSβ and upstream β-oxidative pathway genes; expression of α subunit is a promising control point
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inactive alone; requires equimolar β subunit for activity; alpha subunits can form functional heterodimers with several phylogenetically distant β subunits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhBSα was identified by purification/proteomics as one subunit of petunia benzaldehyde synthase. 2. PhBSα alone is inactive but with PhBSβ forms an active peroxisomal heterodimer that specifically reduces benzoyl-CoA to benzaldehyde using NADPH. 3. Its expression pattern matches benzaldehyde emission, suggesting transcriptional control by the α subunit. 4. Silencing of PhBSα together with PhBSβ strongly decreased benzaldehyde synthase activity and benzaldehyde emission in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: As part of a heterodimeric benzaldehyde synthase with PhBSα, enables NADPH-dependent reduction of benzoyl-CoA to benzaldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; expression pattern suggests less transcriptional specificity than α subunit
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pollinator attractant; flavor volatile; antifungal compound
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Floral volatile emission; internal benzaldehyde pool mainly in cuticle in petunia flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required with PhBSα for benzaldehyde formation; co-silencing reduces benzaldehyde synthase activity and benzaldehyde emission
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in all tissues examined; moderate fluctuations during development and light/dark cycle; does not correlate with benzaldehyde emission
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broad constitutive expression in petunia tissues examined
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Partial purification; proteomics identification; recombinant protein reconstitution with α subunit; enzyme assay; pull-down; Y2H; BiFC; VIGS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Daily light/dark cycle; floral developmental stage
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Co-silencing with PhBSα alters flux to related benzenoid/phenylpropanoid volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary partner for metabolic engineering of benzaldehyde biosynthesis together with α subunit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inactive alone; only single β-subunit gene found in Petunia genus; some cross-species β subunits differ in compatibility with α subunits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhBSβ was co-identified with PhBSα in active purified fractions of petunia benzaldehyde synthase. 2. PhBSβ alone is catalytically inactive but with PhBSα forms an active peroxisomal benzaldehyde synthase specific for benzoyl-CoA. 3. Its transcript pattern is broad and constitutive, unlike the α subunit. 4. Co-silencing with PhBSα reduced enzyme activity and benzaldehyde emission in flowers.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: With AtBSβ forms heterodimeric enzyme reducing benzoyl-CoA to benzaldehyde using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pollinator attractant; flavor volatile; antifungal compound
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers; internal benzaldehyde and benzylalcohol pools in Arabidopsis flowers were below detection but activity present in flower extracts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for Arabidopsis benzaldehyde synthase activity; knockdown reduces BS activity and benzylalcohol/benzaldehyde production in flower extracts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers with relatively low expression in leaves; expression clusters with β-oxidation genes KAT1 and AAE12
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein reconstitution; enzyme assay; T-DNA mutant analysis; expression database analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression/proteome database mining: ePlant; Arabidopsis PeptideAtlas
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports conservation of benzaldehyde synthase for engineering across species
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtBSα formed active hybrids with multiple β subunits from other species
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtBSα with AtBSβ forms an active recombinant benzaldehyde synthase. 2. Arabidopsis AtBSα is highly expressed in flowers and co-expression patterns link it to β-oxidation genes. 3. T-DNA knockdown lines show reduced BS activity and reduced benzylalcohol/benzaldehyde formation in flower extracts, supporting in planta function.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: With AtBSα forms heterodimeric enzyme reducing benzoyl-CoA to benzaldehyde using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pollinator attractant; flavor volatile; antifungal compound
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers; detected indirectly via crude extract activity and benzylalcohol formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for Arabidopsis benzaldehyde synthase activity; knockdown reduces BS activity and benzylalcohol/benzaldehyde production in flower extracts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers with relatively low expression in leaves; expression clusters with β-oxidation genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein reconstitution; enzyme assay; T-DNA mutant analysis; expression database analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Downregulation of AtBSβ resulted in upregulation of AtBSα
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression/proteome database mining: ePlant; Arabidopsis PeptideAtlas
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important conserved partner subunit for engineering benzaldehyde biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis β subunit showed weak compatibility with some non-native α subunits in cross-species assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtBSβ is the partner subunit required with AtBSα for recombinant benzaldehyde synthase activity. 2. It is expressed mainly in flowers and associated with β-oxidation-related expression patterns. 3. T-DNA knockdown lines reduce BS activity and benzylalcohol/benzaldehyde production in flower extracts. 4. Cross-species assays showed AtBSβ can be a weak partner with some foreign α subunits.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: With PdBSβ forms heterodimeric enzyme reducing benzoyl-CoA to benzaldehyde using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, flowers, and young fruits were stated to accumulate high benzaldehyde in almond
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with PdBSβ confers benzaldehyde synthase activity in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homolog cloning/synthesis; recombinant protein reconstitution; enzyme kinetics; cross-species heterodimer assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering natural almond-like benzaldehyde production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PdBSα formed highly active hybrids with several β subunits, but activity depended on β-subunit origin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PdBSα and PdBSβ together form an active benzaldehyde synthase in vitro. 2. The almond enzyme shows benzoyl-CoA reductase kinetics similar to petunia BS. 3. PdBSα can interact with heterologous β subunits, with activity depending on the partner used.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: With PdBSα forms heterodimeric enzyme reducing benzoyl-CoA to benzaldehyde using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required partner for almond benzaldehyde synthase activity in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homolog cloning/synthesis; recombinant protein reconstitution; enzyme kinetics; cross-species heterodimer assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful partner gene for benzaldehyde metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Forms productive hybrids with multiple α subunits from other species
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PdBSβ is required with PdBSα for almond benzaldehyde synthase activity. 2. It supports an enzyme with kinetics comparable to petunia BS. 3. PdBSβ can form active hybrid enzymes with several α subunits from other species.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: With SlBSβ forms heterodimeric enzyme reducing benzoyl-CoA to benzaldehyde using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with SlBSβ confers benzaldehyde synthase activity in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homolog cloning/synthesis; recombinant protein reconstitution; enzyme kinetics; cross-species heterodimer assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower catalytic efficiency than petunia BS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for modifying tomato fruit aroma through benzaldehyde biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Hybrid activity depends strongly on β-subunit partner
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlBSα and SlBSβ together form an active tomato benzaldehyde synthase in vitro. 2. The tomato enzyme has lower catalytic efficiency than petunia BS. 3. SlBSα can form hybrid heterodimers with some foreign β subunits, with strongly partner-dependent activity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: With SlBSα forms heterodimeric enzyme reducing benzoyl-CoA to benzaldehyde using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required partner for tomato benzaldehyde synthase activity in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homolog cloning/synthesis; recombinant protein reconstitution; enzyme kinetics; cross-species heterodimer assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partner gene for engineering tomato benzaldehyde aroma
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Forms productive hybrids with several α subunits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlBSβ is required with SlBSα for tomato benzaldehyde synthase activity. 2. It can support productive hybrid enzymes with multiple α subunits. 3. Cross-species partner choice strongly affects activity.
